---
title: "NCAA Women's Soccer Season — Performance Analytics Dashboard"
output: 
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: rows
    vertical_layout: fill
    theme: bootstrap
runtime: shiny
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(flexdashboard)
library(shiny)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(lubridate)
library(slider)
library(gt)
library(plotly)
library(ggbeeswarm)

# 1. Load Data
df_clean <- read_csv("training_data_anon.csv") %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  mutate(
    start_date   = mdy(start_date),
    session_load = as.numeric(session_load),
    distance_yds = as.numeric(distance_yds),
    hi_events    = as.numeric(no_of_high_intensity_events),
    # Logic to match your original regex extraction for session tags
    session_tag = case_when(
      str_detect(str_to_lower(tags), "md-0") ~ "MATCH",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(tags), "md-1") ~ "MD-1",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(tags), "md-2") ~ "MD-2",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(tags), "md-3") ~ "MD-3",
      str_detect(str_to_lower(tags), "md-4") ~ "MD-4",
      TRUE ~ "Untagged"
    ),
    # Ensure chronological order in plots
    session_tag = factor(session_tag, levels = c("MD-4", "MD-3", "MD-2", "MD-1", "MATCH", "Untagged")),
    session_type = ifelse(session_tag == "MATCH", "Match Session", "Training Session")
  ) %>%
  filter(session_load > 10)

roster_map <- read_csv("roster_map_anon.csv") %>% 
  mutate(athlete_name = str_to_lower(str_trim(athlete_name)))

df_minutes <- read_csv("minutes_data_anon.csv") %>%
  mutate(
    start_date   = as.Date(start_date),
    athlete_name = str_to_lower(str_trim(join_name))) %>%
  select(athlete_name, start_date, minutes)

# 2. Process Metrics
df_with_rolling <- df_clean %>%
  mutate(athlete_name = str_to_lower(str_trim(athlete_name))) %>%
  left_join(roster_map, by = "athlete_name") %>%
  left_join(df_minutes, by = c("athlete_name", "start_date")) %>%
  arrange(athlete_name, start_date) %>%
  group_by(athlete_name) %>%
  mutate(
    chronic_load = slide_index_dbl(session_load, .i = start_date, .f = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE), 
                                   .before = days(28), .complete = FALSE),
    acute_load   = slide_index_dbl(session_load, .i = start_date, .f = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE), 
                                   .before = days(7), .complete = FALSE),
    acwr         = acute_load / chronic_load
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    load_status = case_when(
      acwr > 1.3  ~ "High",   # Matching original logic threshold (~1.2-1.3)
      acwr < 0.8  ~ "Below",
      TRUE        ~ "Normal"
    )
  )

# Visual Branding
color_high   <- "#990000" 
color_normal <- "#000000" 
color_below  <- "#808080"

```

Sidebar {.sidebar}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Dashboard Filters
```{r}

selectInput("unit_choice", "Position Group:", 
            choices = c("All", unique(roster_map$unit)), selected = "All")

dateRangeInput("date_range", "Date Range:",
               start = max(df_with_rolling$start_date, na.rm=T) - days(7),
               end = max(df_with_rolling$start_date, na.rm=T))

checkboxGroupInput("session_filter", "Session Type:",
                   choices = c("Training Session", "Match Session"),
                   selected = c("Training Session", "Match Session"))
```

### Key Metrics

* A:C Ratio: Acute Load (7-day avg) / Chronic Load (28-day avg)
* HI Events: Moments of high-intensity
* HI Density: HI Events normalized by distance (per 1000 yards)
* Density: Intensity per 1000 yards
* Timeline: Vertical red lines indicate Game Days.
* "Heavy" Movers: Players with high Load but low HI events, fatigue indicator

Row {data-height=100}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Total Athletes Tracked

```{r}
renderValueBox({
  count <- df_with_rolling %>% 
    filter(unit == input$unit_choice | input$unit_choice == "All") %>% 
    pull(athlete_name) %>% n_distinct()
  valueBox(count, icon = "fa-users")
})
```

### Average Session Load

```{r}
renderValueBox({
  avg_val <- df_with_rolling %>%
    filter(unit == input$unit_choice | input$unit_choice == "All",
           start_date >= input$date_range[1],
           start_date <= input$date_range[2],
           session_type %in% input$session_filter) %>%
    summarise(avg_load = mean(session_load, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    pull(avg_load)
  
  valueBox(round(coalesce(avg_val, 0), 1), caption = "Avg Session Load", icon = "fa-tachometer-alt", color = "primary")
})

```

Row {data-height=600 .tabset .tabset-fade}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### 7-Day Training Load Table

```{r}

render_gt({
  df_with_rolling %>%
    filter(
      session_type == "Training Session",
      unit == input$unit_choice | input$unit_choice == "All",
      start_date >= input$date_range[1],
      start_date <= input$date_range[2]) %>%
    group_by(athlete_name, unit) %>%
    summarise(
      Sessions    = n(),
      Acute_Avg   = mean(acute_load,  na.rm = TRUE),
      Chronic_Avg = mean(chronic_load, na.rm = TRUE),
      AC_Ratio    = mean(acwr,         na.rm = TRUE),
      Max_Speed   = max(as.numeric(top_speed_mph), na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop") %>%
    arrange(desc(AC_Ratio)) %>%
    gt() %>%
    tab_header(title = "Workload Ratio Analysis", subtitle = "Selected period vs 28-day chronic baseline") %>%
    data_color(
      columns = AC_Ratio,
      palette = c("#63be7b", "#ffffff", "#f8696b"),
      domain = c(0.7, 1.5)) %>%
    fmt_number(columns = -c(athlete_name, unit, Sessions), decimals = 1) %>%
    opt_stylize(style = 1, color = "gray")
})


```

### Daily Load Timeline

```{r}
renderPlotly({
  plot_data <- df_with_rolling %>%
    filter(unit == input$unit_choice | input$unit_choice == "All",
           start_date >= input$date_range[1],
           start_date <= input$date_range[2])
  
  match_dates <- plot_data %>% filter(session_tag == "MATCH") %>% distinct(start_date)
  
  p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = start_date, y = session_load)) +
    geom_vline(data = match_dates, aes(xintercept = start_date), 
               color = color_high, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.4) +
    geom_quasirandom(aes(color = load_status, text = paste("Athlete:", athlete_name, "<br>Load:", round(session_load,1))), alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("High" = color_high, "Normal" = color_normal, "Below" = color_below)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(x = "Date", y = "Session Load")
  
  ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")
})
```


### Intensity Density (HI Events per 1000 Yards)

```{r}

renderPlotly({
  df_trend <- df_with_rolling %>%
    filter(start_date >= input$date_range[1],
           start_date <= input$date_range[2],
           unit == input$unit_choice | input$unit_choice == "All",
           session_tag %in% c("MD-2", "MD-1", "MATCH"))

  p <- ggplot(df_trend, aes(x = session_tag, y = hi_events / (distance_yds / 1000), fill = session_tag)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.4) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("MATCH" = color_high, "MD-1" = "grey", "MD-2" = color_normal)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "HI Density (HI Events / 1k Yds)", x = "Match Proximity", y = "Density")
  
  ggplotly(p)
})
```

### Volume vs. Intensity 
```{r}
renderPlotly({
  df_mech <- df_with_rolling %>%
    filter(!unit %in% c("Goalkeepers"),
           start_date >= input$date_range[1],
           start_date <= input$date_range[2],
           session_type %in% input$session_filter)

  p <- ggplot(df_mech, aes(x = hi_events, y = session_load, color = session_type)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linetype = "dashed", size = 0.5) +
    facet_wrap(~unit) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Match Session" = color_high, "Training Session" = "black")) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(x = "High Intensity Events", y = "Total Session Load")
  
  ggplotly(p)
})
```

Row {data-height=220}
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Understanding the Volume vs. Intensity Map {data-padding=15}

```{r}
wellPanel(
  style = "background-color: #ffffff; border-left: 5px solid #317eac; padding: 15px;",
  HTML("
    <div style='display: flex; gap: 20px;'>
      <div style='flex: 1;'>
        <h4 style='margin-top:0;'>The Relationship</h4>
        <p>This chart plots <strong>High Intensity (HI) Events</strong> against <strong>Session Load</strong>. 
        In fresh athletes, these move together: as explosive actions increase, the total physical cost (Load) rises.</p>
        <strong>Grey Shaded Zone:</strong> Represents the 'Expected Cost.' Players inside this zone are performing at the positional average.
      </div>
      
      <div style='flex: 1; border-left: 1px solid #ddd; padding-left: 20px;'>
        <h4 style='margin-top:0;'>How to Identify Fatigue</h4>
        <ul style='padding-left: 15px;'>
          <li><span style='color: #28a745;'><strong>High Efficiency (Low Right):</strong></span> High explosive output with a relatively low physical cost. Indicates 'fresh legs', or extreme fitness. </li>
          <li><span style='color: #28a745;'><strong>Normal Relationship (Following Line):</strong></span> The athlete is moving efficiently and as expected; their total work is translating into explosive power.</li>
          <li><span style='color: #d9534f;'><strong>The 'Heavy' Mover (High Left):</strong></span> <strong>RED FLAG.</strong> High Load with very few HI events. This suggests the body is working hard but cannot produce explosive power—a primary sign of fatigue.</li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </div>
  ")
)
```



### Recent Session Summary Table

```{r}
render_gt({
  df_with_rolling %>%
    filter(start_date >= input$date_range[1],
       start_date <= input$date_range[2],
       unit == input$unit_choice | input$unit_choice == "All") %>% 
    group_by(athlete_name, unit) %>%
    summarise(
      Sessions   = n(),
      Total_Load = sum(session_load,  na.rm = TRUE),
      Avg_HI     = mean(hi_events,    na.rm = TRUE),
      Max_Speed  = max(as.numeric(top_speed_mph), na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop") %>%
    arrange(desc(Total_Load)) %>%
    gt() %>%
    cols_label(
      athlete_name = "Athlete",
      unit         = "Position",
      Total_Load   = "Total Load",
      Avg_HI       = "Avg HI Events",
      Max_Speed    = "Top Speed") %>%
    fmt_number(columns = c(Total_Load, Avg_HI, Max_Speed), decimals = 1) %>%
    opt_stylize(style = 1, color = "gray")
})
```
